---
title: "tidyposterior"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
#| include: false
#| 
library(googleCloudStorageR)
gcs_global_bucket('bgg_data')

renv::install('tidyposterior', prompt = F)
library(tidyposterior)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r functions}
#| include: false

source(here::here("src/data/load_from_gcs.R"))
source(here::here('src/models/assess.R'))

```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}

prediction_files = 
    gcs_list_objects() |>
    filter(grepl("predictions$", name))

predictions = 
    map_df(
        prediction_files$name,
        ~ gcs_qread(.x)
    )

predictions_tidy = 
    predictions |>
    separate_wider_delim(
        cols = object_name,
        names = c("bucket",
                  "project", 
                  "type", 
                  "branch"),
        too_few = 'align_start',
        too_many = 'drop',
        delim = "/"
    ) |>
    unite(bucket:branch,
          col = object_name,
          sep = "/")

```

```{r}

reg_metrics = yardstick::metric_set(
    yardstick::rmse,
    yardstick::rsq
)

metrics = 
    predictions_tidy |>
    filter(usersrated >= 25) |>
    group_by(object_name) |>
    nest() |>
    mutate(data = map(data, pivot_outcomes)) |>
    unnest(data) |>
    group_by(outcome, yearpublished, object_name) |>
    assess_outcomes(
        metrics = reg_metrics
    )

```

```{r}

temp = 
    metrics |>
    mutate(object_name = gsub("bgg_data/bgg_models/model/", "", object_name)) |>
    select(object_name, outcome, yearpublished, .metric, .estimate) |> 
    filter(.metric == 'rmse') |>
    filter(outcome == 'average') |>
    mutate(id = paste(outcome, yearpublished, sep = "-")) |>
    select(id, object_name, .estimate) |>
    pivot_wider(
        names_from = c("object_name"),
        values_from = c(".estimate")
    )

set.seed(1)
perf = perf_mod(temp, iter = 3000)

```
```{r}

perf %>% 
    tidy() %>% 
    ggplot(aes(y=model, 
               x = posterior))+
    ggridges::stat_density_ridges(
        quantile_lines = T,
        quantiles = 1,
        quantile_fun = median,
        alpha = 0.5,
        fill = 'deepskyblue1',
        color = 'black'
    )+
    theme_minimal()

```

```{r}

contrast = 
    contrast_models(perf)

contrast |>
    summary() |>
    mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 3)

contrast_models(perf) |>
    autoplot() +
    theme_minimal()

```
